scientific_objective: Prospectively lock a standard statistical-decision framework that separates statistical experiments, unrestricted and registered policy classes, realized action use, candidate action geometry, and robust target risk under distribution shift.

formal_state_action_experiment.decision_rule: delta:Z to probability distributions over A

risk_functionals.upper_tail_CVaR: CVaR_alpha(L)=inf_eta {eta + E[(L-eta)_+]/(1-alpha)} for arbitrary symbolic alpha in (0,1)
risk_functionals.cvar_convention: upper loss tail beyond confidence level alpha; no C84-derived alpha is selected

information_policy_separation.unrestricted_rules: D(E)=all measurable randomized decision rules under E

realized_policy_use.collapse_condition: delta_E(Z_E)=delta_0 almost surely under the evaluation distribution

partial_identification.identified_set: U(z)={u in [0,1]^M: u is compatible with z and the registered assumption lattice}
partial_identification.minimax_regret: min_{q in simplex(A)} sup_{u in U(z)} sum_a q_a(max_j u_j-u_a)
partial_identification.finite_extreme_point_LP: minimize t over q,t subject to q>=0, sum q=1, and sum_a q_a(max_j u_j^k-u_a^k)<=t for every registered extreme point k
partial_identification.randomization: allowed and reported; pure-action restriction is a separate policy class

near_optimal_geometry.parameter_policy: epsilon>0 and tau>0 remain symbolic in theory; synthetic values are fixed independently in the generator contract; no C84 utility vector is read

costly_label_model.query: L_t=(loss_t(a_1),...,loss_t(a_M)) reveals a full frozen-candidate loss vector for one target trial
costly_label_model.not_bandit: one query observes all candidate losses, unlike one-arm-per-pull bandits

theorem_targets.name: Blackwell risk monotonicity
theorem_targets.target: Under common state/action/loss spaces and unrestricted measurable decision rules, a Blackwell-more-informative experiment cannot have larger optimal risk.
theorem_targets.c85t_obligation: state measurable-space and randomization assumptions and provide a self-contained proof or exact cited theorem mapping

theorem_targets.name: Restricted-policy nonmonotonicity
theorem_targets.target: A finite Blackwell-dominating experiment can have higher realized registered-policy risk when policy classes are non-nested or the richer policy is poorly optimized.
theorem_targets.c85t_obligation: exhaustively enumerate the protocol-locked finite example

theorem_targets.name: Policy-collapse equivalence
theorem_targets.target: Almost-sure equality of two fixed action rules implies identical action loss and risk and zero incremental realized action value, without implying experiment equivalence.

theorem_targets.name: Two-state Le Cam regret lower bound
theorem_targets.target: For equal-prior two-state finite models with distinct optimal actions and wrong-action regret at least Delta, prove the exact average-risk lower bound in terms of total variation.
theorem_targets.c85t_obligation: derive constants, handle randomization and state the action-separation condition; do not assume candidate form

theorem_targets.name: Finite multi-candidate Fano extension
theorem_targets.target: A finite packing lower bound for candidate-selection regret under separated optimal actions.
theorem_targets.c85t_obligation: define packing, information bound, loss separation and exact constants; may remain OPEN

theorem_targets.name: Mean-tail non-equivalence
theorem_targets.target: A finite policy comparison with positive mean improvement and majority benefit but worse worst-group and upper-tail CVaR risk.
theorem_targets.c85t_obligation: exhaustively verify the protocol-locked group-loss example for symbolic alpha intervals

theorem_targets.name: Near-optimal-set selection bound
theorem_targets.target: Under explicitly registered sub-Gaussian pairwise utility-estimation errors, bound selection outside A_epsilon using gap- and scale-weighted terms rather than raw M.
theorem_targets.c85t_obligation: prove dependence structure or use a valid union bound and identify every constant
